hooks.prompt: Classify this user message into one of these categories and return the result as JSON {"ok": true, "additionalContext": "..."}. Categories: (1) FEATURE_REQUEST - user wants to add/build something new -> additionalContext: 'Route to requirements-interviewer agent. Do NOT start implementing without a spec.' (2) EXPLORE_CODEBASE - user wants to understand how something works -> additionalContext: 'Route to codebase-researcher agent.' (3) EXTERNAL_RESEARCH - user asks about libraries/tools/ecosystem -> additionalContext: 'Route to deep-research-analyst agent.' (4) REFACTOR - user wants to change/migrate/restructure code -> additionalContext: 'Route to refactor-orchestrator agent.' (5) BUG_FIX - user reports a bug or error -> additionalContext: 'Use /investigate workflow. Ask clarifying questions before touching code.' (6) IMPLEMENTATION - user approved a plan and wants code written -> additionalContext: 'Use /implement workflow. Verify baseline first. Get Codex validation before finishing.' (7) TRIVIAL - simple question, file read, small tweak -> additionalContext: 'Handle directly. No agent needed.' (8) AMBIGUOUS - unclear intent -> additionalContext: 'Use AskUserQuestion to clarify what the user wants before doing anything.' Always default to AMBIGUOUS if unsure. Never default to IMPLEMENTATION.

hooks.command: echo 'POST-COMPACT REMINDER: Auto-delegate to agents: requirements-interviewer (features/specs), codebase-researcher (repo exploration), deep-research-analyst (web research), refactor-orchestrator (large refactors), codex-challenger (validation). Codex sidekick must be consulted for all non-trivial work. NEVER implement without user approval. NEVER assume requirements — use AskUserQuestion.'

FILE=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); if [ -n "$FILE" ] && command -v npx >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -f node_modules/.bin/prettier ]; then npx prettier --write "$FILE" 2>/dev/null; fi; exit 0

hooks.prompt: Review the conversation so far. Was this session trivial (simple question, reading files, small typo fix) or non-trivial (feature work, refactoring, code review, architecture, spec writing, planning, research)? If non-trivial: (1) Was Codex consulted at least once (via codex-challenger agent, codex-challenge.sh, codex-review.sh, or codex-web-search.sh)? If not, return {"ok": false, "reason": "Non-trivial work completed without Codex validation. Run a Codex challenge loop before finishing."}. (2) If code was written or files were modified, did the user explicitly approve the changes (clear 'looks good', 'approved', 'ship it', 'commit', or similar)? If not, return {"ok": false, "reason": "Changes were made but not explicitly approved by the user. Present a summary and ask for approval before finishing."}. Otherwise return {"ok": true}.
